tasks.name: implement-lint-fix-max-params
tasks.task:
Fix all local/max-params violations (functions with more than 4 parameters) in the following files:
- src/cli/components/parts/PipelineRunner/usePipelineRun.ts
- src/domains/pipeline.ts
- src/services/pipelineService.ts

For each violating function, choose the most appropriate refactoring:
1. Group tightly related parameters into a named options object type (preferred when parameters represent a single concept).
2. Split the function if it has too many responsibilities.

Define new object types using `type` (not interface). Keep the public API consistent — update all call sites if a signature changes (use mcp__perclst__ts_get_references to find them). After editing, run `./node_modules/.bin/eslint src/cli/components/parts/PipelineRunner/usePipelineRun.ts src/domains/pipeline.ts src/services/pipelineService.ts` to verify no violations remain.

tasks.name: review-lint-fix-max-params
tasks.task:
Review the local/max-params refactoring applied to:
- src/cli/components/parts/PipelineRunner/usePipelineRun.ts
- src/domains/pipeline.ts
- src/services/pipelineService.ts

ng_output_path: .claude/tmp/review-lint-fix-max-params

Check:
1. Parameter groupings are semantically coherent (names make sense together).
2. New object types are well-named and use `type` (not interface).
3. All call sites have been updated consistently.
4. No behavior change — the refactoring is purely structural.
5. No violations of the project layer architecture (no cross-layer imports).

If issues are found, write them to ng_output_path. If everything is correct, do not write the file.

tasks.command: npm run build && npm run test:unit

tasks.name: implement-lint-fix-max-params
tasks.task: Refactoring complete, review passed, and build/tests passing. Commit the changes with an appropriate conventional commit message (e.g. refactor(lint): reduce parameter counts in pipeline and hook functions).
